principle: Classify each provider as a set of independently routed capability fractions. A provider can belong to multiple swarms through different fractions.
rules: A provider may have many fractions.
A fraction may belong to many swarms.
Different fractions of one provider may carry different risk and SEAL levels.
Only the fraction required for a task should be invoked.
A fraction receives no access outside its declared data and delivery scope.
Financial tokenization or ownership division is a separate governed process and is not implied by functional fractionalization.

questions: Which human and AI roles participate?
What is the failure and fallback route?
What cost, latency and quota apply?
What SEAL level governs the fraction?

classes.interface: human-facing app, dashboard, chat, voice or visual surface
classes.human-service: human action remains part of delivery
classes.physical: delivery occurs through a person, device or physical system
classes.proof-only: delivery is a receipt, verification, timestamp or attestation
